ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for instant noodles with chicken nuggets as a combined dish; closest parent is instant noodle food product (FOODON_00002473).
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for Mama instant noodles with chicken nuggets; closest parent is cooked noodles (A0FJP). Chicken nugget component not separately coded.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT food concept found for this combined instant noodle and chicken nugget dish.

main_ingredients: Mama instant noodles (wheat flour, palm oil, seasoning sachet)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving of Mama instant noodles with chicken nuggets is approximately 380 g, comprising a cooked block of Mama brand instant noodles in seasoned broth topped with several deep-fried chicken nuggets.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Chicken nuggets, frozen, unprepared (FDC ID 168320) and Noodles, Chinese, chow mein, dry (FDC ID 20113); combined weighted estimate for restaurant-style instant noodle dish with chicken nuggets
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry for Mama instant noodles with chicken nuggets exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or FAO/INFOODS SEA. Values are estimated by combining USDA FoodData Central data for cooked instant noodles (approximately 60% by weight) and deep-fried chicken nuggets (approximately 40% by weight), adjusted for typical restaurant serving composition. Mama brand noodle nutritional label data (per Thai FDA requirements) used to cross-check carbohydrate and sodium values. Confidence is medium due to estimation.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. Instant noodles (GI ~47–58, mean ~52–55 referenced to glucose).
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Mama brand instant noodles with chicken nuggets as a combined dish. GI of 55 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care) for instant noodles (wheat-based, boiled), which report GI values in the range 47–58 (mean ~52–55, glucose reference). A GI of 55 is classified as 'low'. The chicken nugget component contributes negligible additional glycemic load. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (55 × 85.5) / 100 ≈ 47.0, where carbohydrate per 380 g serving = 22.5 × 3.8 = 85.5 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Very high sodium content from instant noodle seasoning sachet and broth
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this dish due to its estimated sodium content of approximately 520 mg per 100 g (approximately 1,976 mg per standard 380 g serving), which represents a substantial proportion of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2,000 mg; requesting reduced seasoning or consuming only part of the broth can meaningfully lower sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load from refined wheat noodles and large carbohydrate portion
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should exercise caution with this dish as the estimated glycemic load per standard serving is approximately 47, driven by the high carbohydrate content of refined wheat instant noodles; portion reduction, pairing with non-starchy vegetables, and choosing lower-GI noodle alternatives are recommended strategies to moderate postprandial blood glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium, saturated fat from deep-fried nuggets, and trans fat from palm oil in instant noodles
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of this dish due to its combination of high sodium (approximately 1,976 mg per serving), saturated fat from deep-fried chicken nuggets, and residual trans fat from palm oil used in instant noodle frying; occasional consumption with broth reduction and smaller portions is advisable.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Mama Instant Noodles with Chicken Nuggets is a Thai-origin fast-casual dish served at restaurants such as Sanook Kitchen in Singapore. It consists of a cooked block of Mama brand instant noodles — Thailand's most iconic instant noodle brand, made from wheat flour and palm-oil-fried noodle cakes — served in a seasoned chicken broth made from the included flavour sachet, topped with several deep-fried breaded chicken nuggets. The noodles are soft and springy with a savoury, slightly spicy broth, while the chicken nuggets add a crispy, protein-rich contrast. The dish is ultra-processed due to the instant noodle component and the pre-formed nuggets. Nutritionally, it is energy-dense at approximately 195 kcal per 100 g, with a moderate protein content of 8.5 g per 100 g from the chicken nuggets, significant carbohydrate from the noodles (22.5 g per 100 g), and notably high sodium (approximately 520 mg per 100 g) from the seasoning sachet. The glycemic index is estimated at 55 (low), and the glycemic load per standard 380 g serving is approximately 47, making it a high-GL meal. Clinically, this dish raises concern for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and cardiovascular disease due to its sodium load, refined carbohydrate content, and saturated fat from frying. It is generally considered halal and dairy-free but contains gluten and soy allergens.

confidence_reason: No direct nutritional entry for this specific combined dish (Mama instant noodles with chicken nuggets) was found in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, FAO/INFOODS SEA, or USDA FoodData Central as a single record. Values are estimated by weighted combination of USDA FoodData Central entries for cooked instant noodles and deep-fried chicken nuggets, cross-checked against Mama brand label data. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database for wheat-based instant noodles. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data (cooked instant noodles ~60% weight, deep-fried chicken nuggets ~40% weight) and cross-checked against Mama brand Thai FDA nutritional label data. No single approved-source entry for this exact dish was available. 2. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reliably reported across the component sources used. 3. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary USDA component entries used for estimation. 4. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary USDA component entries used for estimation. 5. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the primary USDA component entries used for estimation. 6. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this food type. 7. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary USDA component entries used for estimation. 8. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary USDA component entries used for estimation. 9. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care) for wheat-based instant noodles; no direct GI measurement for this combined dish exists. A GI of 55 is classified as 'low'. 10. The food_group 'Meat and alternatives' is preserved from the input record as the chicken nugget component is the primary protein source; however, this dish is mixed in nature. 11. Halal status is assumed based on Mama brand Thailand halal certification and typical chicken nugget preparation at Thai-cuisine restaurants in Singapore; individual restaurant verification is recommended. 12. The Chinese name 妈妈方便面配鸡块 reflects the brand name 妈妈 (Mama) as referenced in the input food_name.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Constructed full record from scratch. Canonical food_name assigned. Thai local name added in Thai script. Chinese simplified and traditional added reflecting Mama brand name. Nutrients estimated from USDA FoodData Central weighted component method. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008. All -1 sentinel values documented in notes. Ontology codes assigned as closest_parent with gap notes. Health context completed for all three required conditions. LLM description written to exceed 200 characters. Aliases consistency verified between aliases_by_language and flat aliases.

Iteration 2: Corrected gi_category from 'medium' to 'low' for glycemic_index 55, as per standard GI classification (GI <= 55 is low). Updated gi_source.source_match_notes and llm_training.natural_language_description to reflect this change. The high concern level for Type 2 Diabetes remains appropriate due to the high glycemic load (GL 47.0) despite the low GI.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: PASS — all hard gates passed and the record scored 8/8.
